vocalization.primary_call: ki-ki-ki or kee-kee-kee rapid staccato chatter

conservation.trend: stable / urban expansion in some regions

theory_nodes.concept: FALCON CLOSE (3rd of 4). Degree 233 closes the three-degree Falconidae core run (231-232-233). Kestrel (234) is the final family member.
theory_nodes.description: The Merlin's structural role in the Falconidae arc.

theory_nodes.concept: Size-to-assertiveness ratio: Merlin is small (125-300g) but attacks crows, ravens, and large raptors. The ki-ki-ki rapid chatter is the voice of this disproportionate assertiveness.

Black Merlin (suckleyi): darkest North American Merlin, resident on wet Pacific coast WA-CA. The PNW-specific subspecies adds a year-round resident dimension beyond the winter visitor taiga population.
theory_nodes.description: The black Merlin as PNW resident subspecies.

cross_references.relationship: Falconidae opens. Largest PNW falcon. 4x heavier than Merlin. Same family, very different scale.
Final Falconidae entry. Kestrel = smallest North American falcon. The family closes one degree after the Merlin.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=5+6=11. Compact, concentrated energy: rapid ki-ki-ki chatter + dense fuzz-reverb guitar. Both deliver impact in compressed form. Love Battery return.

milestones: Black Merlin suckleyi = PNW resident subspecies
